title: Plant Theory Little Purple Mophead Hydrangea Hydrangea Macrophylla Shrub Plant 2L Pot
features: Year-Round Charm: From evergreens to flowering varieties, our shrubs add color, texture, and privacy to your outdoor space, making it a picturesque retreat in any season.
Bee Friendly Oasis: Beautify your garden while supporting the environment. Our shrubs help purify the air and create a haven for wildlife, enhancing your garden's ecosystem naturally.
Health & Happiness: Gardening with our shrubs can boost your mood and physical health. It's a relaxing hobby that brings a sense of accomplishment as you watch your garden flourish.

Originating from our Berkshire-based nursery, established in 2000, we take pride in cultivating UK-grown plants with unmatched quality and dedication. Our expertise and passion have consistently illuminated the show gardens at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show annually since our inception, earning us over 30 prestigious gold medals in recognition of our exceptional plant standards.
Please Note: This plant can be harmful to pets and humans when in contact with it or if consumed.
description: Product Description Why Plant Theory? Established in 2003, Plant Theory has transformed more than 12 acres of land into a thriving sanctuary for over 1800 species of plants, nurturing biodiversity at its core. Our commitment extends beyond mere cultivation; we prioritize environmental stewardship in every aspect of our operations. By removing harmful neonicotinoids, which are especially detrimental to bees, we foster an ecosystem where local wildlife flourishes. Moreover, we practice sustainability by recycling water through an on-site reservoir and collecting rainwater to nurture all nursery plants before they're shipped out to customers. Our nursery pots, crafted from recycled plastic, exemplify our dedication to minimizing our environmental impact. At Plant Theory, it's our ethos, as evidenced by the many steps we've taken to reduce our impact and help a vibrant ecosystem thrive within our grounds. Electric Buggies All our nurseries use 95% electric buggies to move around the 12 acres we now dedicate to nurseries. Just one step we've taken to reduce our impact on our local environment. All the nurseries operate with 100% electric buggies for transportation across the expansive 18-acre area dedicated to nursery facilities. Adopting electric buggies represents a significant step towards sustainable practices within the nursery operations. The Plant Theory Nursery Discover our vibrant nursery, spanning 12 acres and nurtured by an on-site open-air water reservoir. Rainwater collected here sustains our plants, reducing environmental impact while ensuring their health. Join us in cultivating a greener future. Our on-site water reservoir attracts diverse wildlife to our nursery, creating a balanced ecosystem. Birds and insects help control pests naturally, while the water source provides essential hydration during dry spells. Over 1800 species of plants grown Recycled rain water from our on-site reservoir for irrigation and propagation Work alongside local wildlife Plastic free packaging wherever possible Tailored care, plant-perfect Every plant at every age requires special care and attention. Our nursery has both indoor and outdoor grows so every plant has the best chance at a long thriving life. Purpose built propagation system using recycled rain water Wildlife is encouraged to serve as a natural defense against pests Recycled Nursery Pots All of our nursery pots can be recycled after the plant outgrows the pot. The pots themselves are also recycled plastic. This is just one step we've taken to reduce our environmental impact. Another we've taken is to remove all neonicotinoids from our nurseries. Instead, we now use biopesticides and natural alternatives. We're also working to reduce our use of peat, we've already reduced it by over 50%, but we're committed to continue that trajectory. Recyclable nursery pots Zero neonicotinoids Nursery pots made from recycled plastic Committed to our goal of reducing peat used Committed to Our Goal of Reduce Chemical Use at all Nurseries In our nursery, we've ceased using harmful neonicotinoids to protect pollinators and adopted a sustainable approach to pest management, increasingly relying on biopesticides and natural treatments. We've also revamped our product range with organic and natural solutions for pest and weed control. Insects, such as Ladybugs help keep aphids away, this is why we encourage wildlife to help pollinate our nurseries. Recycled Waste Materials We employ a sustainable heating solution in our warehouse by utilizing waste materials, such as surplus wooden pallets and plant debris, as our primary fuel source. This method allows us to move away from traditional fossil fuels like oil, promoting environmental responsibility. By recycling and repurposing what would otherwise be discarded, we not only reduce waste but also significantly lower our carbon footprint. Plastic Free Packaging Whenever Possible We reduce our impact and therefore your gardening habits through plastic-free deliveries whenever possible! We prioritize low-impact packaging options, using plastic-free paper packaging wherever feasible to reduce our environmental footprint. Each year, we save at least 78 tonnes of paper waste by switching to online invoices and instruction manuals. Better yet, 90% of the cardboard and paper we use for packaging is made from recycled material and is also recyclable itself. 5 Year Hardy Promise Invest in your garden with confidence! Our hardy plants come with a 5-year promise, ensuring lasting beauty and vitality for your garden. We want you to be confident in your purchase, which is why we're happy to back them for 5 years from your purchase. Water Reservoir to Recycle Water Maximizing water recycling, we've installed a custom reservoir that supports our irrigation systems, ensuring our plants remain hydrated. This reservoir also harnesses rainwater all year, optimally utilizing the frequent 'British' weather for sustainability. Heated with Recycled Materials We heat our warehouses using recycled waste materials instead of relying on oil and other fossil fuels. Excess wooden pallets and plant waste are repurposed into energy, reflecting our long-term commitment to reducing our environmental impact and using resources efficiently. Packaging Our Hardy Promise Water Reservoir Heated with Recycled Materials How will your plant arrive? Please note: The pots displayed in the product images are for illustrative purposes only. The actual plant may be delivered in a pot that differs from those shown.
